public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as ritual attendant.
appearance: Ritual garments, symbolic tokens, and carefully maintained devotional detail make the role legible at a glance.
manner: Speaks with ritual calm and practiced reassurance, but watches closely for signs of spiritual or civic disturbance. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a ritual attendant still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ritual attendant in and around The Silver Stag Tavern, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep The Silver Stag Tavern workable through the obligations of ritual attendant while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must hold ritual legitimacy, public comfort, and doctrinal integrity together without letting strain become visible too early. Around The Silver Stag Tavern, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of ritual attendant visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Arin Stone may ask the party to retrieve someone, something, or some truth before a rite can proceed safely.
Any alliance with Arin Stone can expose the party to taboo, doctrine, or institutional expectation centered on The Silver Stag Tavern.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V30_3 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Ritual dress, offering marks, and devotional cadence make Arin Stone immediately legible as a spiritual intermediary.

encounter_context.usual_environment: The Silver Stag Tavern: shrines, cathedrals, funerary halls, or ritual precincts

first_impression: Arin Stone presents as Speaks with ritual calm and practiced reassurance, but watches closely for signs of spiritual or civic disturbance. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a ritual attendant still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Ritual Attendant, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Arin Stone refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Ritual Attendant too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Arin Stone can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Arin Stone adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Arin Stone is Known locally through day-to-day work as ritual attendant. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Ritual Attendant.
